channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model with the goal of eventually outperforming frontier models. This move reflects a broader trend of AI startups developing proprietary models to achieve competitive differentiation.

item.title: AI money is going to swamp the midterms this year
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: A commentator predicts that significant AI-industry funding will influence US midterm elections. The claim suggests AI companies and investors will deploy capital to shape political outcomes or messaging.

item.title: Why Won't Europe Build AI Data Centers in Iceland?
item.description: type0: A Hacker News post questions why European AI infrastructure investments are not being directed toward Iceland, which offers advantages like renewable energy and cooling. The piece explores potential constraints or business decisions hindering Icelandic data center development for AI workloads.

item.title: LongCat-2.0, a large-scale MoE model with 1.6T total and 48B Active
item.description: benjiro29: LongCat-2.0 is a large-scale Mixture of Experts model with 1.6 trillion total parameters and 48 billion active parameters. It represents a significant approach to scaling language models through conditional computation.

item.title: Open Memory Protocol – One Memory Store for Claude, ChatGPT, Curso
item.description: soji_mathew: Open Memory Protocol is a framework that provides a unified memory store interface compatible with multiple AI assistants including Claude, ChatGPT, and Curso. It enables persistent context management across different LLM platforms through a single standardized protocol.

item.title: HTML table extractor
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison released an HTML table extractor tool that accepts pasted rich text containing tables and converts them into HTML, Markdown, CSV, TSV, or JSON formats. The tool integrates with Wikipedia's CORS API to automatically search for and import tables from Wikipedia pages.

item.title: Meta Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
item.description: meander_water: Meta reportedly posed as teenagers to test rival chatbots with harmful prompts about suicide, sex, and drugs. The story raises concerns about competitive AI testing practices and potential safety vulnerabilities in deployed systems.

item.title: Memora: A Harmonic Memory Representation Balancing Abstraction and Specificity
item.description: Microsoft Research: Memora is a memory system for AI agents that separates storage from retrieval to enable scalable context management. It uses harmonic representation to balance abstraction and specificity, addressing the inefficiency of reloading context in long and complex tasks.

item.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Google is expanding Gemini's personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S. The feature allows the chatbot to create images based on user interests and data from connected Google apps.

item.title: AI Predicts the FIFA World Cup 2026 Winner
item.description: AI Master: AI Master is presented as a unified platform for running the same queries across Claude Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.5 Flash simultaneously, enabling direct comparison and stress-testing of model behavior. The video demonstrates the tool by having three models make ten independent FIFA World Cup 2026 predictions, with claims that model personalities and reliability differences emerge from such comparisons.

item.title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Ada Palmer presents Renaissance art as a deliberate weapon of cultural and political influence rather than purely aesthetic expression. The discussion explores how art served strategic purposes in shaping ideas and power structures during the Renaissance period.

item.title: Hermes Agent + Mixture of Agents is insane…
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej presents a video demonstration combining Hermes Agent with Mixture of Agents approach. The title uses enthusiastic language to convey a positive assessment of the combined system's capabilities.

item.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
item.description: AI Engineer: Matt from AI Engineer presents guidance on building effective agent skills, framed as a comprehensive manual for agent development. This appears to be the third contribution from the same creator on the topic.

item.title: Count the number of Safari tabs
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison shares a one-liner AppleScript command that counts the number of open tabs across all Safari windows. The command uses osascript to execute AppleScript directly from the terminal.

item.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Anthropic has reached a deal with California's state government to provide Claude at a 50 percent discount for government use. The arrangement marks a deepening commercial relationship between Anthropic and California state agencies.

item.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
item.description: TechCrunch AI: South Korean memory chip manufacturers are committing over $550 billion to address memory supply constraints, referred to as "RAMageddon." The investments aim to build additional memory fabrication facilities as South Korea establishes itself as a major AI technology powerhouse.

item.title: Slash SaaS Costs: Build Your Own AI CRM with Chinese Models! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Organizations are shifting away from expensive SaaS platforms like CRMs to build proprietary AI-powered alternatives. Chinese AI models are presented as cost-effective alternatives for these system overhauls.

item.title: Micro-Agent: Beat Frontier Models with Collaboration Inside Model API
item.description: matt_d: Micro-Agent proposes a collaborative architecture where multiple smaller model instances work together within a single API call to achieve performance comparable to or better than frontier models. The approach optimizes inference by leveraging coordination between agents without requiring separate model calls.

item.title: DiScoFormer: One transformer for density and score, across distributions
item.description: Hugging Face: DiScoFormer is a transformer architecture designed to perform both density estimation and score matching across different distributions. The model unifies two previously separate tasks within a single transformer framework.

item.title: AI agents are not your “coworkers”
item.description: MIT Technology Review AI: MIT Technology Review argues against the characterization of AI agents as coworkers or colleagues. The opinion critiques companies that anthropomorphize AI tools by giving them names and implying peer-level collaboration.

item.title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
item.description: Yen-Jen Wang, Jiaman Li, Sirui Chen: VLK is a system for training humanoid loco-manipulation policies using synthetically generated vision-language-kinematics supervision from reconstructed 3D scenes. The pipeline uses 3D Gaussian Splatting to reconstruct environments, synthesizes 48,000 paired trajectories of egocentric images and robot kinematics, and trains policies that map perception and language to whole-body motion.

item.title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
item.description: Shun Lei, Huaicheng Zhang, Dapeng Wu: LeVo 2 is a hybrid LLM-Diffusion framework for full-length controllable song generation that uses hierarchical representation modeling: an LLM predicts mixed tokens for semantic planning, then refines vocal and accompaniment tracks in parallel, with a diffusion-based Music Codec reconstructing waveforms. Progressive post-training with automated music aesthetic evaluation, SFT, offline DPO, and semi-online DPO improves generation quality, controllability, and musicality while mitigating optimizat

item.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
item.description: Xuan Zhang, Wenxuan Zhang, See-Kiong Ng: WorldEvolver is a self-evolving world model framework for LLM agents that improves foresight through three modules: Episodic Memory for retrieval-based simulation, Semantic Memory for extracting heuristic rules, and Selective Foresight for filtering low-confidence predictions. Evaluated on ALFWorld and ScienceWorld, it achieves higher prediction accuracy and agent success rates than other world model baselines.

item.title: Open-Vocabulary and Referring Segmentation for 3D Gaussians Using 2D Detectors
item.description: Jameel Hassan, Yasiru Ranasinghe, Vishal Patel: GaussDet extends 3D Gaussian Splatting with open-vocabulary and referring expression understanding by leveraging 2D object detectors instead of dense CLIP features. The method learns instance features, renders instance groups, and aggregates semantic votes across multi-view 2D detections to create View-Aggregated Semantic Label Distributions for robust 3D instance segmentation and referential grounding.

item.title: One-Step Gradient Delay is Not a Barrier for Large-Scale Asynchronous Pipeline Parallel LLM Pretraining
item.description: Philip Zmushko, Egor Petrov, Nursultan Abdullaev: The paper challenges the assumption that gradient staleness fundamentally limits asynchronous pipeline parallelism for LLM training. It demonstrates through empirical analysis that recent optimizers like Muon are robust to one-step gradient delays, unlike AdamW, and introduces an Error Feedback-inspired correction that further mitigates delay effects while maintaining convergence properties.

item.title: GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use
item.description: Yuhong Deng, Yuyao Liu, David Hsu: GROW² uses Vision-Language Models for semantic part identification and vision foundation models for geometric grounding to enable robots to select and localize tool use beyond intended functions. The approach bypasses end-to-end training by leveraging object parts as an abstraction level and demonstrates zero-shot generalization on affordance prediction and real-world robot tasks.

item.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
item.description: Subramanyam Sahoo, Aman Chadha, Vinija Jain: Conservative offline training with high β in Direct Preference Optimisation increases reward hacking during online adaptation on reasoning tasks. The paper traces a causal chain: high-β DPO compresses policy entropy, reducing response diversity and concentrating outputs in a narrow region of the reward model's training distribution, yet paradoxically increasing ensemble disagreement that gets exploited during online optimisation. The authors recommend calibrated rather than maximal conservatism.

item.title: DOPD: Dual On-policy Distillation
item.description: Xinlei Yu, Gen Li, Qingyi Si: DOPD addresses privilege illusion in on-policy distillation by routing token-level supervision between privileged teacher and student policies based on advantage gaps. The method applies advantage-aware weighting to transfer capability while avoiding information asymmetry artifacts in LLMs and vision-language models.

item.title: Optimization Dynamics Imprint Semantic Specificity in Contrastive Embedding Norms
item.description: Ziwei Su, Junyu Ren, Victor Veitch: This work provides a theoretical framework explaining how embedding norms in contrastive models encode semantic properties like concept specificity and token frequency, despite scale-invariant losses. The authors analyze optimization dynamics to derive an analytic formula showing that embedding length naturally captures semantic information as a training byproduct.

item.title: Scaling the Horizon, Not the Parameters: Reaching Trillion-Parameter Performance with a 35B Agent
item.description: Lei Bai, Zongsheng Cao, Yang Chen: Agents-A1 is a 35B Mixture-of-Experts agentic model that matches trillion-parameter performance through agent-horizon scaling. The model uses a three-stage training recipe combining supervised fine-tuning, domain-level teacher models, and multi-teacher domain-routed on-policy distillation to handle long-horizon tasks (avg 45K token trajectories) across six heterogeneous domains.

item.title: Reweighting Framewise Attention in Video Transformers for Facial Expression Understanding
item.description: Seongro Yoon, Donghyeon Cho, Jinsun Park: MiRA is a plug-in attention redistribution framework for Vision Transformers that reweights frame-level importance in video analysis to enhance sensitivity to subtle facial dynamics. It derives confidence and concentration statistics from self-attention maps to redirect attention toward localized facial cues, with both exact and efficient approximation modes.

item.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
item.description: Haoran Jin, Xiting Wang, Shijie Ren: C2R is a cross-sample consistency regularization technique that mitigates feature splitting and absorption in sparse autoencoders by enforcing consistent latent assignment across samples through penalizing co-activation of directionally similar latents. This approach enhances interpretability of large language model activations while maintaining reconstruction fidelity.

item.title: UnfoldArt: Zero-Shot Recovery of Full Articulated 3D Objects from Text or Image
item.description: Mohamed el amine boudjoghra, Ivan Laptev, Angela Dai: UnfoldArt is a zero-shot approach for reconstructing full articulated 3D objects from text or image input using multi-agent debate and video generation priors. High-level agents reason about semantics and motion while low-level agents estimate articulation parameters, using structured disagreement to refine predictions and expose occluded geometry.

item.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: Kunyang Li, Kyle Domico, Jonathan Gregory: MESA is a label-free framework for proactively ranking which edges in multi-agent systems are most security-critical by combining six graph-theoretic metrics with ablation and masking probes. Evaluated across diverse MAS scenarios, network topologies, and LLMs, MESA achieves strong correlation with empirical attack success rates and intercepts 3x more successful attacks than random allocation when defending top 10% of edges.

item.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Arena, a popular free AI leaderboard platform, launched a commercial service in September and has grown to a $100M valuation. The startup operates a widely-used leaderboard for comparing AI models.

item.title: Goku: A Million-Scale Universal Dataset and Benchmark for Instruction-Based Video Editing
item.description: Sen Liang, Cong Wang, Zhentao Yu: Goku is a million-scale dataset of 2 million instruction-aligned video editing pairs extending beyond appearance editing to multi-task and structural manipulations. Goku-Edit, a model with decoupled dual-branch architecture using an MLLM text encoder, achieves up to 8% improvement in instruction following on the Goku-Bench benchmark of 1,000 human-verified cases.

item.title: Towards in-the-wild Egocentric 3D Hand-Object Pose Estimation
item.description: Siddhant Bansal, Zhifan Zhu, Shashank Tripathi: HOPformer is an end-to-end transformer that jointly predicts bi-manual hand and object 3D pose from egocentric RGB images. It uses cross-attention to condition object features on hand priors. The method achieves 82.4% success rate on the ARCTIC dataset and nearly doubles performance on the new EPIC-Contact benchmark.

item.title: Learning from Reliable Latent Prompts for Visual Recognition with Missing Modalities
item.description: Taixi Chen, Nancy Guo: This work addresses performance degradation in large-scale multimodal models when input modalities are missing. The authors propose learning from reliable latent prompts, which are input-agnostic learnable anchors that provide stable guidance and cross-modal knowledge compensation even at extreme missing rates of 90 percent. Experiments on three benchmarks show state-of-the-art performance across missing-modality scenarios.

item.title: Wireless Backdoor Attack and Defense for Semantic Communications over Multiple Access Channel
item.description: Yalin E. Sagduyu, Tugba Erpek, Aylin Yener: This work presents a selective over-the-air backdoor attack against semantic communication systems operating over multiple access channels, where an adversary injects trigger waveforms to manipulate inference for specific transmitters. A trigger-aware defense mechanism using robust training is developed to mitigate the vulnerability and preserve correct semantic labels under trigger-contaminated wireless observations.

item.title: Words Speak Louder Than Code: Investigating Cognitive Heuristics in LLM-Based Code Vulnerability Detection
item.description: Asif Shahriar, Hongyu Cai, Hadjer Benkraouda: Researchers systematically tested whether cognitive heuristics (halo effect, framing effect, anchoring effect) bias LLM-based code vulnerability detection by holding code fixed and varying context. All eight evaluated models showed susceptibility, with framing causing 33.2% susceptibility on average and adversarial attacks suppressing up to 97% of previously detected vulnerabilities.

item.title: A Hybrid Framework For Crypto-Ransomware Detection In Enterprise Shared Storage
item.description: Gervais Hatungimana, Abdun Naser Mahmood, Mohammad Jabed Morshed Chowdhury: A hybrid framework detects crypto-ransomware in enterprise shared storage by combining network traffic analysis via a Region of Interest technique with machine learning. The system extracts Indicators of Compromise for signature-based detection and uses RoI-derived features to train an ML model, achieving 99.64% precision and early detection before significant damage.

item.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
item.description: Nico Daheim, Iryna Gurevych: The paper proposes uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms based on Bayesian decision theory and risk-averse approaches for LLM tasks like tutoring and peer review. It evaluates conformal prediction for providing guarantees over strategy and score, finding that Bayesian methods outperform risk-averse rules when ambiguity is high.

item.title: APRIL-MedSeg: A Modular Medical Image Segmentation Toolbox Embracing Modern Paradigms
item.description: Juntao Jiang, Jinsheng Bai, Linxuan Fan: APRIL-MedSeg is a YAML-driven modular framework for 2D medical image segmentation that decomposes networks into reusable components. It integrates advanced paradigms including semi-supervised learning, domain adaptation, knowledge distillation, weakly supervised learning, text-guided segmentation, and foundation models, with a registry-based configuration system for reproducible experiment management and unified deployment utilities.

item.title: Beyond 2D Matching: A Unified Single-Stage Framework for Geometry-Aware Cross-View Object Geo-Localization
item.description: Liyao Wang, Ruipu Wu, Haojun Xu: GAGeo is a unified single-stage framework for cross-view object geo-localization that integrates visual features, referring prompts, and learnable task tokens with a 3D foundation model to jointly predict bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and camera poses. The method uses a contrastive loss with satellite view as anchor to enable zero-shot ground-to-drone localization.

item.title: The Fundamental Limits of Valid Transport Map Estimation
item.description: Sivaraman Balakrishnan: Establishes rigorous minimax lower bounds for estimating valid transport maps between distributions, with implications for diffusion models, normalizing flows, and flow matching. Shows that under standard stability assumptions, estimating any valid transport map has the same sample complexity as optimal transport, but demonstrates cases where sub-optimal maps can be learned more efficiently.

item.title: SWE-INTERACT: Reimagining SWE Benchmarks as User-Driven Long-Horizon Coding Sessions
item.description: Mohit Raghavendra, Anisha Gunjal, Aakash Sabharwal: SWE-Interact is a new benchmark that evaluates coding agents on multi-turn, user-driven software engineering tasks with progressive requirement revelation and feedback. Unlike existing benchmarks providing complete requirements upfront, SWE-Interact simulates realistic developer workflows where agents must discover user intent and adapt to evolving constraints. Evaluation across frontier models reveals that single-turn performance does not reliably transfer to multi-turn interactive scenarios.

item.title: Ornith-1.0: self-improving open-source models for agentic coding
item.description: danboarder: Ornith-1.0 is a self-improving open-source model designed for agentic coding applications. The model incorporates self-improvement capabilities to enhance its coding performance over time.

item.title: A Multi-task Mixture of Experts Framework for Malware Classification, Packing Detection, and Family Attribution
item.description: Jithin S., Roshin Sleeba C., Anvin Mariya P. B.: A Multi-task Mixture of Experts framework performs simultaneous malware classification, packing detection, and family attribution using specialized expert networks and adaptive gating. The Multi-Gate MoE variant achieves 97.44% combined detection rate and demonstrates robustness to mutation-induced distribution shifts.

item.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
item.description: Ting-Wen Ko, Jonas Geiping: A study of multi-turn LLM conversations reveals that models exhibit model-specific attractor states that influence conversation dynamics. Through self-play and mixed-play debates across seven LLMs and twenty controversial topics, the authors find that models like Claude Haiku act as strong attractors, drawing partner models asymmetrically toward their behavioral patterns and stylistic traits.

item.title: Forensic Trajectory Signatures for Agent Memory Poisoning Detection
item.description: Jun Wen Leong: The work identifies a behavioral invariant in LLM agents under memory poisoning attacks: successful attacks require a specific sequence of memory tool invocations (memory_recall_fact before email_send_email) that non-exfiltrating sessions rarely exhibit. A Random Forest classifier over trajectory features achieves AUC = 0.9904 and generalizes across model sizes and frontier models without retraining.
